rationale: The citing note says the 2017 study extends prior banner-advertising research that mainly examined online outcomes, and the cited 2015 paper is precisely a randomized display-advertising study focused on site-visit effects across purchase-funnel stages. Their core constructs strongly overlap (display/banner exposure, website visits, segment/stage heterogeneity), making this a plausible theory/benchmark foundation rather than an incidental mention. With a direct DOI match (10.1509/jmr.13.0277), this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
